topic: Claude Opus 4.7 Developer Guide: xhigh Effort, Task Budgets, and Migration

key_points: Complete API setup guide with model identifier claude-opus-4-7
Covers task budgets with beta header task-budgets-2026-03-13
Explains xhigh effort as Claude Code default on all plans (Pro, Max, Teams, Enterprise)
Documents tokenizer changes: 1.0x-1.35x more tokens vs Opus 4.6
Lists all availability: Claude.ai, API, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, Snowflake Cortex, GitHub Copilot
Benchmarks: SWE-bench Verified 87.6%, SWE-bench Pro 64.3%, MCP Atlas 79.1%
Vision: max image resolution increased to 2576px / 3.75MP (from 1568px / 1.15MP)
xhigh effort: new tier between high and max, better reasoning without full max cost
Migration path: switch from thinking.type 'enabled' to 'adaptive', remove budget_tokens
Three breaking changes: budget_tokens removed, temperature/top_p/top_k removed, adaptive-only thinking
Task budgets are advisory (soft cap), not enforced hard caps like max_tokens
Minimum task budget: 20,000 tokens; recommended range for coding: 50,000-128,000 tokens
Prompt caching minimum cacheable prompt: 4,096 tokens
New economics of agentic billing: task budgets prevent runaway reasoning charges
Model sees a running token countdown and gracefully wraps up as budget is consumed
Practical developer perspective: do not rush migration without benchmarking tokenizer impact
Validate prompts for literal interpretation changes before full rollout
xhigh effort delivers most of max reasoning depth at lower token cost
budget_tokens removal returns HTTP 400 — most impactful breaking change in production
Adaptive thinking: model decides reasoning allocation per prompt context
xhigh sits between high and max on the effort scale (low, medium, high, xhigh, max)
/ultrareview feature introduced alongside xhigh in Opus 4.7 Claude Code
Task budgets cover full agentic loop: thinking + tool calls + tool results + final output
Agentic coding tasks trend 50k-128k token budgets; targeted refactors trend lower
Task budgets require beta header opt-in: task-budgets-2026-03-13

stats.fact: Claude Opus 4.7 was released on April 16, 2026
Pricing unchanged from Opus 4.6: $5/M input tokens, $25/M output tokens
Claude Opus 4.7 scores 87.6% on SWE-bench Verified — ahead of GPT-5.5 and DeepSeek V4 Pro (55.4%)
Claude Opus 4.7 scores 64.3% on SWE-bench Pro vs GPT-5.5 at 58.6%
Claude Opus 4.7 scores 79.1% on MCP Atlas tool-use benchmark
Max image resolution increased to 2576px / 3.75MP — up from 1568px / 1.15MP in Opus 4.6
Task budget minimum is 20,000 tokens; recommended range for agentic coding tasks is 50,000-128,000 tokens
New tokenizer uses 1.0x to 1.35x more tokens vs Opus 4.6 depending on content type (code sees up to 1.35x)
Prompt caching reduces cached reads to $0.50/M tokens (90% savings); Batch API provides 50% discount
Claude Code requires v2.1.111 or later to use Opus 4.7 with xhigh default effort
Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4, and Opus 4 retire on June 15, 2026 — migration deadline
GPT-5.5 outperforms Claude Opus 4.7 on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (82.7% vs 69.4%) for CLI automation

angles: From zero to production: complete API setup guide for Claude Opus 4.7 — model ID, SDK version, beta headers
xhigh effort explained: the new tier between high and max, when to use it, and what it actually costs vs alternatives
Task budgets as the key to predictable agentic billing — no more runaway reasoning charges on long agentic loops
The three breaking changes that will break your Opus 4.6 production code without warning (400 errors)
Adaptive thinking deep dive: why removing budget_tokens improves reasoning quality for most workloads
Vision upgrade: 3.75MP vs 1.15MP and what multimodal coding workflows become possible
Migration checklist: test tokenizer impact, validate prompts, roll out incrementally before the June 15 deadline
Benchmark reality check: where Opus 4.7 leads (SWE-bench, MCP tool use) and where GPT-5.5 still wins (Terminal-Bench)

how to use Claude Opus 4.7 API 2026
